title: ranunculus neapolitanus subsp. tommasinii
intro: Ranunculus neapolitanus subsp. tommasinii, also known as Tommasini's buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus neapolitanus plant, a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tommasini's buttercup include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. Tommasini's buttercup can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, with some cultures also using it in traditional remedies for various health issues.
